Sanctification Discipline  

Description

1: Increase Chance to Resist Spell by 10000%

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 0 Skill: Defense
Casting Time: Instant Recast Time: 4050
Fizzle Time: 0 Range: 200
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Hate Generated: 1 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: No Interruptable: No
Short Buff Box: No Timer: 3
Target Type: Self Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Melee Flags

Initial Endurance Cost:0 Endurance Cost:55/second
Opening Types:
  • Triggered on demand

Messages

Cast on you: Your body is surrounded in an aura of sanctification.
Cast on other: Soandso is surrounded in an aura of sanctification.
Effect Fades: Your sanctification fades.

Game Description

Surrounds your body in an aura of sanctification, turning aside most detrimental spells.

Items with this effect

  • Tome of Sanctification Discipline

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Tome of Sanctification Discipline
ClassLevel
PAL60
Expansion:

Duration:
3 ticks
  • Comments (12)
  • Sold By (9)
  • Screenshots

where do you get this from?
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2006 at 11:24 PM Rating: Default
Otternaut
292 posts
found on Ulin Velnick in PoK library for 23+pp
▲ Page top
Death Touch
QuoteReply
# Dec 19 2004 at 11:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592254578068
15 posts
For those that are wondering if this disc will stop Cazic Touch https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/spell.html?spell=982 aka death touch, it will not. Death touch is setup to be unresistable. How Sony makes this unresistable is it checks for a whopping -32k against your base saves and if you can't meet that, which you wont be able to, you take 32k damage. Our sanctification Discipline only adds a 10k positive modifier to our base save (more info can be found under the raw spell data link above). We would need this disc to stack 3 if not 4 times over to resist an unresistable spell.

This disc is great though as it will resist the majority of spells that come our way. Just Sony did a good job making sure that spells that are marked as unresistable stayed unresistable.

Edited, Sun Dec 19 23:13:44 2004
▲ Page top

Resist ALL spells?
QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2004 at 9:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785342788
29 posts
I think once I get this discipline, I will try and see if it works against mobs that use Death Touch. Would be nice to run in and be immune to the DT spell and start whacking the mob right away. PLUS...if this does make you immune to DT, you can make it safe for the rest of a raid by having the mob use the Death Touch spell and start the timer for the re-use of DT...becuase all mobs that use Death Touch can use it every so many ticks...not exactly sure though how often a mob can use Death Touch. But, those are my thoughts.

QuoteReply
# Dec 19 2003 at 10:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257862009
31 posts

▲ Page top
RE: This prolly on test
QuoteReply
# Dec 11 2003 at 10:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966302447
View User Forum
Scholar
172 posts
Paladins already have disciplines on the live servers and Sanctification is the one we get at level 60. The other disciplines we get are:

Resistant (raises all resistances for 15 seconds)
Fearless (unable to be feared for 15 seconds)
Holyforge (chance to crit against undead for 5 minutes)

The big question us pally's have is whether these will be linked to the upcoming melee changes so that these disciplines can be triggered more than once every 72 minutes and from what this new info is showing apparently it IS linked to our Endurance bar. WOOT!

-Bael

Edited, Thu Dec 11 10:21:10 2003
▲ Page top
RE: This prolly on test

__DEL__1595175497525
6 posts

__DEL__1591789791776
Scholar
21 posts
It does increase your chance to score a critical hit, but not by much. However, it does give you the chance to score a Crippling Blow as well, but the CB is only on Undead. *smiles*

# Jul 13 2004 at 11:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591797730523

"It does increase your chance to score a critical hit, but not by much. However, it does give you the chance to score a Crippling Blow as well, but the CB is only on Undead. *smiles*" you dont use it much if you think that i got my first CB ever useing this on a non undead and it sorta works backwards will crit 2x as much on on undead then undead. but thats not even what this thread is about ... i'll soon know if using sanc disc can resist fully aa'd HT from SK :-) hope it does lol
